summary: Final re-verification of 2 unresolved fixes from iteration_6. Fix 5 (Recharts warnings) PASSES on both mobile 390x844 and desktop 1280x800. Fix 1 (logout → '/') STILL FAILS on both viewports — URL ends at /login?from=%2Finfluencer instead of '/'. Root cause is NOT InfluencerLayout; it is ProtectedRoute.jsx wrapping the layout and rendering <Navigate to=`/login?from=...`/> synchronously the moment user becomes null. The signingOutRef inside InfluencerLayout never gets a chance to take effect because ProtectedRoute unmounts the layout first.

ui_bugs.issue: After logout, URL is '/login?from=%2Finfluencer' on BOTH desktop sidebar logout ([data-testid='influencer-logout']) and mobile topbar logout ([data-testid='influencer-topbar-logout']). The landing hero h1 is NOT visible — instead the login page h1 'Welcome back' is shown. The fix added a signingOutRef inside InfluencerLayout, but InfluencerLayout is rendered as a child of <ProtectedRoute>. ProtectedRoute reads `user` from useAuth() and on null synchronously returns <Navigate to={`/login?from=${encodeURIComponent(location.pathname)}`} replace />. So when logout() sets user=null and React re-renders, ProtectedRoute returns Navigate first — the layout (and its signingOutRef + navigate('/')) never run.

action_items: Move the logout-redirect logic OUT of the layout and into a place that fires BEFORE ProtectedRoute can intercept. Direct fix: in AuthContext.logout, accept an optional redirect target and call window.location.replace('/') (or navigate('/', {replace:true}) via a stored navigator) BEFORE clearing the user state. Alternative: in ProtectedRoute.jsx, check a module-level `isSigningOut` flag (or sessionStorage key 'brandkrt:signingOut') and, when true, return <Navigate to='/' replace /> instead of '/login?from=...'. Set the flag in the influencer logout handler BEFORE await logout() and clear it after the navigation completes.
critical_code_review_comments: ProtectedRoute.jsx unconditionally redirects unauthenticated users to /login?from=<path>. This is correct for direct URL hits but wrong during an explicit logout flow. Add a logout-aware bypass (module-level flag or context property like isSigningOut).

InfluencerLayout.jsx still contains the (now ineffective) signingOutRef + useEffect bypass. Once the ProtectedRoute fix lands, decide whether to keep this as defense-in-depth or remove for clarity. The same pattern likely needs to be applied to BrandLayout and AdminLayout if they share ProtectedRoute.

success_rate.frontend: 50% (Fix 5 PASS on both viewports; Fix 1 FAIL on both viewports)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Fix 5 (Recharts) is DONE — width(-1) height(-1) warning is gone on both mobile and desktop /influencer/earnings after the ResponsiveContainer was given a numeric height={280} prop directly. Fix 1 (logout → /) is still broken because the redirect is owned by /app/frontend/src/components/ProtectedRoute.jsx, not InfluencerLayout. Any patch must touch ProtectedRoute or AuthContext so it knows about explicit logouts. Verified logout DOES log the user out (token cleared, /login shown) — only the destination URL is wrong.

rca of the issue.fix1_logout_redirect: ROOT CAUSE: /app/frontend/src/components/ProtectedRoute.jsx wraps InfluencerLayout. Lines 15-17 return <Navigate to={`/login?from=${encodeURIComponent(location.pathname)}`} replace /> whenever `user` is null. When the logout handler calls `await logout()`, AuthContext sets user=null → React re-renders the ProtectedRoute → ProtectedRoute returns the Navigate component → React Router commits the URL change to /login?from=%2Finfluencer → InfluencerLayout (and its signingOutRef.current=true + navigate('/')) is UNMOUNTED before its finally block can navigate. REPRO: login as demo.influencer / Demo@12345, click [data-testid='influencer-logout'] (desktop) or [data-testid='influencer-topbar-logout'] (mobile@390x844), observe URL → /login?from=%2Finfluencer (FAILS — should be '/'). MITIGATION (recommended): add a top-level signingOut flag in AuthContext: `const signingOutRef = useRef(false); const logout = async (redirectTo='/') => { signingOutRef.current = true; ...clear state; window.location.replace(redirectTo); };` — using window.location.replace bypasses React Router entirely so ProtectedRoute never gets to render Navigate. Alternative: expose `signingOut` from AuthContext and have ProtectedRoute check it before issuing the /login redirect.
rca of the issue.fix5_recharts_warning:
RESOLVED. Switching from
to a direct eliminated the width(-1) height(-1) warning. Verified zero matching console warnings on both 390x844 and 1280x800 after fresh page load + 3s settle.
